data.title: Avista receives approval of all-party, all issues settlement in Idaho general rate cases
data.description:
• Avista (NYSE:AVA) has received approval from the Idaho Public Utilities Commission of the all-party, all issues settlement agreement that was filed on June 9, 2025, concluding the company’s electric and natural gas general rate cases, the company said on Friday.
  • New electric rates take effect Sept. 1, 2025 and Sept 1, 2026.
  • The approved rates are designed to increase annual base electric revenues by $19.5 million or 6.3%, effective Sept. 1, 2025, and by $14.7 million or 4.5%, effective Sept. 1, 2026.
  • For natural gas, the settlement agreement is designed to increase annual base natural gas revenues by $4.6 million or 9.2%, effective Sept. 1, 2025, and reduce base revenues by $0.2 million or 0.4%, effective Sept. 1, 2026.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ScanTech AI receives Nasdaq deficiency letter
data.description:
• ScanTech AI Systems (NASDAQ:STAI) announced on Friday that the company received a Nasdaq letter, stating that the company is not in compliance with the Nasdaq Listing Rule as a result of not having timely filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission its quarterly report on Form 10-Q for the quarterly period ended June 30, 2025. 
  • The company said the notice has no immediate effect on the listing of the company’s common stock on Nasdaq.
  • The notice stated that the company has 60 calendar days to submit a plan to regain compliance. 
  • STAI +1.93% after hours to $0.431.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Jacobio Pharmaceuticals Group reports 1H results
data.description:
• Jacobio Pharmaceuticals Group press release (OTCPK:JBPHF): 1H Revenue of RMB45.7M.
  • The net loss for the first half of 2025 was RMB59.0 million,  narrowing by 65.1% compared with the same period in 2024.

data.title: SU Group Holdings receives Nasdaq notice for public float deficiency
data.description:
• SU Group Holdings (NASDAQ:SUGP) on Friday said it received a deficiency notice from Nasdaq on Aug. 27 for failing to maintain the minimum number of publicly held shares required under Listing Rule 5550(a)(4).
  • Nasdaq determined the company has ~424,000 publicly held shares as of August 25, below the 500,000 minimum threshold. The figure excludes shares held by officers, directors, or 10%+ beneficial owners.
  • The notice has no immediate impact on the company’s Nasdaq listing or trading, which continues under the ticker "SUGP."
  • SU Group has until October 13 to submit a compliance plan and intends to do so within the deadline.
  • SUGP -0.71% after hours to $6.25.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: U.S. drilling rig count edges lower in latest Baker Hughes report
data.description:
The number of total oil and gas drilling rigs in the U.S. fell for the 16th time in 18 weeks, declining by 2 to 536, Baker Hughes said Friday in its latest report.

Oil rigs rose by 1 to 412 during the week, while gas rigs lost 3 to 119 and 5 rigs remained classified as miscellaneous.

The total rig count was down 47 rigs, or 8% compared to the same time last year.

Rigs targeting crude oil in the Permian Basin finished flat at 253, the count in the Eagle Ford gained 1 to 29, and the Williston Basin lost 1 to 28.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

data.title: SF Fed's Daly: Will soon be time to recalibrate policy
data.description:
San Francisco Fed President Mary C. Daly said on Friday it will soon be time to recalibrate policy as the central bank's dual mandates of full employment and price stability are in tension. Tariffs are moving inflation higher, as the labor market is showing signs of slowing, she added.

But she does think that the tariff related price increases will be a one-off, repeating comments she has made in recent weeks.

"It will take time before we know that for certain," she wrote in a post on LinkedIn. "But we can't wait for perfect certainty without risking harm to the labor market."

Recently, traders are pricing in an 88.9% probability of a 25-basis-point rate cut to 4.00%-4.25%, up from a probability of 84.7% a week ago, according to the CME FedWatch tool.

Economists and investors received another update on inflation today, with the release of the July PCE price index, which showed an uptick in year-over-year core PCE, which excludes food and energy. The data indicated that inflation, while still above the Fed's goal of 2%, isn't on a runaway trajectory. Still, it will take time for the full effects of tariffs to be reflected in consumer prices.

Next week, markets will get a fresher read on the jobs market with the release of the Bureau of Labor Statistics' Employment Situation report.

data.title: Frontline turns higher after reporting mixed Q2; offloads oldest suezmax
data.description:
Frontline (NYSE:FRO) +1.2% in Friday's trading, pushing into positive territory after reporting mixed Q2 results, with adjusted earnings missing estimates and revenues that topped expectations, while also expecting spot time charter equivalent earnings for Q3 to come in below spot TCEs currently contracted due to the impact of ballast days during Q3.

Q2 profit of $77.5M, or $0.35/share, fell 59% from $0.84/share in the year-earlier quarter but gained from $0.15/share in Q1, while revenues of $480.1M fell 14% from $556M a year ago but gained 12% from $427.9M in Q1.

Q2 average daily spot time charter equivalent earnings of $43,100 for VLCCs, $38,900 for Suezmax tankers, and $29,300 for LR2/Aframax tankers, with total TCE earnings rising to $283M from $241M in Q1.

The company also announced an agreement to sell its oldest Suezmax tanker, built in 2011, to an undisclosed buyer for $36.4M, which is expected to generate $23.7M in Q3 net cash proceeds and a $6M gain from the sale.

As of the end of Q2, Frontline's (NYSE:FRO) fleet totaled 81 vessels with an average age of 7.1 years, including 22 suezmaxes.

Q3 "rate bookings exclude ballasting days and hence the full TCE for the quarter will be lower, though the bookings screens soft regardless," Fearnley analysts Fredrik Dybwad and Nils Thommesen said in a note, forecasting Q3 earnings coming in ~$0.30/share vs. $0.41/share consensus.

"With the cat out of the bag and rates gaining momentum on the tail-end of 3Q and into Q4, we believe a potential negative reaction in the share price in the coming days [is] an enticing opportunity ahead of what likely will be a much better Q4," the Fearnley analysts wrote.

data.title: California Uber and Lyft drivers cleared to unionize
data.description:
Uber (Uber) and Lyft (LYFT) drivers in California are now able to unionize while still remaining independent contractors after the two companies worked out a deal with Governor Gavin Newsom and state lawmakers.

According to the Los Angeles Times, Newsom, Assembly Speaker Robert Rivas and Senate Pro Tem Mike McGuire will back a compromise that would advance the collective bargaining bill out of the legislature along with a bill sponsored by Lyft (LYFT) and Uber (NYSE:UBER) that would “significantly reduce” both companies’ insurance requirements for ride-share drivers.

Under the previous law, the ride-share companies must carry $1M in coverage per driver for accidents caused by other underinsured or uninsured drivers. Under the current agreement, Uber (NYSE:UBER) and Lyft (LYFT) can instead carry underinsured or uninsured motorist coverage of $60,000 per rideshare driver and $300,000 per accident.

The two bills “represent a compromise that lowers costs for riders while creating stronger voices for drivers,” Uber’s head of public policy for California said in a statement.

For drivers, the agreement allows for negotiated pay, and would exempt the workers from the state and federal anti-trust laws that would prohibit collective action by independent contractors.
